title: Mayor Ben Walsh, department leaders raise concerns over council budget transparency
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh and department heads publicly criticized the Common Council's lack of transparency during budget deliberations, citing an eleventh-hour approval of 80+ amendments cutting $16M from the proposed budget with no prior notice or negotiation. The Council engaged an outside accounting firm ($20,750) whose recommendations were withheld from the administration and public, prompting concerns about accountability and proper governance processes.

political_signal.description: Executive branch openly questioning legislative branch's budget process integrity, transparency standards, and use of external consultants; Council defending autonomy but declining to justify withholding analyst recommendations.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts extracted: $16M (cuts), $3.5M (police cut), $20,750 (accounting firm contract)
Date: May 12, 2025 - budget vote occurred 'last week'
Substantive governance tension between Mayor and Council on procedural accountability
Council's refusal to explain withholding of firm's report suggests potential data governance or deliberative privilege issue relevant to CIO transparency initiatives
